behavior_summary: U8P_WARDEW implements a simple in-game wardrobe item with two process handlers: U8P_WARDEW::look (the examine handler) and U8P_WARDEW::use (the U8P_WARDEW::use/activate handler).
U8P_WARDEW::look displays the short label 'wardrobe' to the player when the item is examined and then suspends/ends the U8P_WARDEW::look interaction.

U8P_WARDEW::use inspects the item's status flags (via Item::getStatus(this)) and uses bit 0x0100 as an inferred indicator of whether the wardrobe's UI (gump) is currently open. If that bit is set it calls Item::closeGump(this); otherwise it calls Item::openGump(0x0D, this). This effectively toggles or opens/closes the wardrobe gump.
notable_control_flow: U8P_WARDEW::look: simple linear flow — emit a bark/string 'wardrobe', then suspend/end the process.

U8P_WARDEW::use: read Item::getStatus(this) into a temporary (local1), test mask 0x0100 in an if-statement; branch to either Item::closeGump(this) (when bit set) or Item::openGump(0x0D, this) (when bit clear).
open_questions: What is the canonical semantic meaning of status bit 0x0100 on this item (inferred as a 'gump open' flag but not confirmed)?
Does gump id 0x0D correspond exactly to the wardrobe UI in all builds/variants, or is it context-dependent?
Are there any other item-level flags or game-wide flags that influence wardrobe behavior outside this snippet (none are read/written here)?

main_function.name: U8P_WARDEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short label/description when the player examines/looks at the wardrobe item.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_WARDEW::look/examine event for the item (player 'U8P_WARDEW::look' or 'examine' action).

function_map.name: U8P_WARDEW::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler that shows the short name 'wardrobe'

function_map.name: U8P_WARDEW::use
function_map.role: handle player 'U8P_WARDEW::use' interactions; toggle or open/close the item's wardrobe gump

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: read item status/flags used to decide gump state

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: open the item's wardrobe UI/gump (called here with id 0x0D)

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's status/bitfield (result stored in a temporary local to test bit 0x0100).
Close the item's UI/gump when the inferred 'gump open' bit (0x0100) is set.
Open the wardrobe UI/gump (gump id 0x0D) when the inferred 'gump open' bit (0x0100) is not set.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WARDEW implements a simple in-game wardrobe item with two process handlers: U8P_WARDEW::look (the examine handler) and U8P_WARDEW::use (the U8P_WARDEW::use/activate handler).
